code: @Manual{,   title = {cdcm: Canonical DCM for Effective Connectivity in fMRI},   author = {Kaitlyn Fales},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/kaitlyn-fales/cdcm}, }

what: CDCM Effective Connectivity Modeling of Task-fMRI Data
title: CDCM Effective Connectivity Modeling of Task-fMRI Data
text: R package Bayesian estimation effective connectivity task-based fMRI using Canonical Dynamic Causal Modeling (CDCM).

title: CDCM Effective Connectivity Modeling of Task-fMRI Data
text: cdcm provides tools : Simulating fMRI time series CDCM framework Constructing Stan data CDCM models Running efficient MCMC sampling via CmdStanR Generating initialization using Pathfinder Supporting reproducible effective connectivity analyses across studies

title: CDCM Effective Connectivity Modeling of Task-fMRI Data
CDCM Effective Connectivity Modeling of Task-fMRI Data
text: R (≥ 4.2 recommended) cmdstanr CmdStan (≥ 2.35) install CmdStan:

title: CDCM Effective Connectivity Modeling of Task-fMRI Data
code: library(cdcm)  # Define true connectivity parameters  nu <- list(   nu_A = c(0.4, 0.3, -0.1, 0.15),   nu_B = c(-0.2),   nu_C = c(0.7) )  # Specify model structure  idxs <- list(   A_idxs = matrix(c(2,1,                     1,2,                     1,1,                     2,2), byrow = TRUE, ncol = 2),   B_idxs = matrix(c(1,2,1), byrow = TRUE, ncol = 3),   C_idxs = matrix(c(1,1), byrow = TRUE, ncol = 2) )  # Simple block design with a single stimulus U <- matrix(rep(c(rep(0,5),rep(1,5)), 10), ncol = 1)  # Simulate data sim <- simulate_cdcm(   nu = nu,   hypothesis_idxs = idxs,   nscan = 100,   m = 2,   n_u = 1,   TR = 2,   U = U,   SNR = 3,   seed = 123 )  dat <- sim$simulated_data  # Compile model mod <- compile_cdcm()  # Prepare data stan_dat <- get_stan_dat(dat, idxs)  # Convergence criterion - Multivariate ESS (95% HPD, 5% tolerance) minESS_check <- minESS_criterion(stan_dat, alpha = 0.05, eps = 0.05)  # Initialize via Pathfinder init <- get_initial_vals(mod, stan_dat)  # Run sampling until multivariate ESS convergence dir.create("Output", showWarnings = FALSE) fit <- dcm_sample(   mod = mod,   data = stan_dat,   inits_list = init,   output_dir = "Output",   basename = "cdcm_fit",   ess_check = minESS_check, )

title: CDCM Effective Connectivity Modeling of Task-fMRI Data
text: Canonical DCM (CDCM) task-based fMRI Meta-analysis model group-level inference Additional models (e.g., multi-run meta-analysis) planned.

what: Check whether a block design satisfies sufficient identifiability diagnostics — check_design_identifiability
title: Check whether a block design satisfies sufficient identifiability diagnostics — check_design_identifiability
text: Evaluates design sufficient conditions identifiability block-design experimental input. checks intended practical design diagnostics CDCM analyses related simulation studies.

title: Check whether a block design satisfies sufficient identifiability diagnostics — check_design_identifiability
Check whether a block design satisfies sufficient identifiability diagnostics — check_design_identifiability
text: data list containing: times: numeric vector observation times u: \(T \times n_u\) matrix encoding experimental stimulus inputs y_obs: \(T \times m\) matrix observed ROI-level BOLD signals warn Logical; TRUE, warnings issued one sufficient conditions satisfied.

title: Check whether a block design satisfies sufficient identifiability diagnostics — check_design_identifiability
text: list containing: T number observations. m number ROIs. n_u number stimulus dimensions. changepoints changepoint vector returned internally get_changepoints(). block_lengths block lengths computed design matrix. first_block_zero Logical indicating whether first input block -zero input configuration. n_distinct_block_inputs number distinct blockwise input configurations observed. cond_A1_first Logical indicating whether first block satisfies minimum block-length requirement. cond_A1_later Logical indicating whether later blocks satisfy minimum block-length requirement. cond_A1 Logical indicating whether block-length condition satisfied overall. cond_A2 Logical indicating whether observation-count condition satisfied. cond_A3 Logical NA. designs n_u = 2 3, indicates whether enough distinct blockwise input settings observed. values n_u, NA. all_ok Logical indicating whether evaluated sufficient-condition diagnostics satisfied.

title: Check whether a block design satisfies sufficient identifiability diagnostics — check_design_identifiability
text: function evaluates set practical design diagnostics motivated sufficient conditions identifiability CDCM framework block-design experiments. particular, checks: whether block lengths long enough relative number ROIs whether total number observations large enough relative number ROIs stimulus inputs whether enough distinct blockwise input configurations observed number stimulus dimensions 2 3 checks based sufficient conditions interpreted practical diagnostics rather necessary conditions. Failure satisfy one necessarily imply non-identifiability, may indicate design weak reliable estimation. number ROIs taken m = ncol(y_obs), number stimulus dimensions n_u = ncol(u), number observations T = length(times).

what: Sample from the CDCM posterior in checkpointed chunks — dcm_sample
title: Sample from the CDCM posterior in checkpointed chunks — dcm_sample
text: Runs posterior sampling compiled CDCM model sequential chunks, saving intermediate results disk chunk stopping target multivariate effective sample size (ESS) reached maximum iteration limit exceeded.

title: Sample from the CDCM posterior in checkpointed chunks — dcm_sample
code: dcm_sample(   mod,   data,   inits_list,   output_dir,   basename,   ess_check,   refresh = 100,   warmup_iter = 5000,   n_iter_chunk = 1000,   max_iter = 10^6,   adapt_delta = 0.9,   seed = 1234 )

title: Sample from the CDCM posterior in checkpointed chunks — dcm_sample
text: mod compiled CmdStanR model object, typically returned compile_cdcm. data named list data format required Stan model, typically produced get_stan_dat. inits_list named list initial values model parameters, typically produced get_initial_vals. output_dir Character string giving directory checkpointed posterior draws diagnostics saved. basename Character string used base filename saved output files. ess_check Numeric scalar giving target multivariate ESS used convergence assessment. refresh Integer giving sampling progress refresh rate passed CmdStanR. warmup_iter Integer giving number warmup iterations initial sampling phase. n_iter_chunk Integer giving number post-warmup sampling iterations run per chunk. max_iter Integer giving maximum total number post-warmup draws allowed across chunks. adapt_delta Numeric target acceptance rate passed CmdStanR. seed Integer random seed reproducibility.

title: Sample from the CDCM posterior in checkpointed chunks — dcm_sample
text: Invisibly returns list elements: draws data frame containing combined posterior draws across completed sampling chunks. diagnostics list NUTS diagnostics completed sampling chunk. converged Logical indicating whether ESS-based convergence criterion satisfied. total_draws Total number post-warmup draws generated across completed chunks. sampling interrupted function completes, checkpointed draws diagnostics saved disk remain available, final return object produced.

title: Sample from the CDCM posterior in checkpointed chunks — dcm_sample
text: function designed checkpointed single-chain sampling especially useful long runs HPC systems, jobs may need stop walltime limits. standard multi-chain sampling, use compiled CmdStanR model directly via mod$sample(). Sampling begins initial warmup phase, followed posterior sampling chunks. first chunk, last draw used initial value next chunk, adapted step size inverse metric previous fit reused sampling can continue efficiently without repeating warmup. completed chunk, posterior draws NUTS diagnostics saved disk output_dir using filenames based basename. checkpointing allows intermediate results recovered sampling interrupted, example due HPC walltime limits. Convergence assessed using multivariate effective sample size (ESS), computed via mcmcse, accumulated single-chain posterior draws. Sampling continues target ESS specified ess_check reached maximum number iterations exceeded. function intentionally restricted single-chain sampling. checkpointing logic, reuse last draw initialization, reuse adapted inverse metric step size, ESS calculation based single continuing chain. multiple chains desired, users call underlying CmdStanR sampler directly via mod$sample() (compiled model object's $sample() method), specifying preferred number chains parallelization settings. case, checkpointed chunkwise workflow implemented used.

title: Sample from the CDCM posterior in checkpointed chunks — dcm_sample
text: standard multi-chain posterior sampling, call compiled CmdStanR model directly rather using dcm_sample(). example: approach provides standard multi-chain CmdStanR sampling, include checkpointing chunkwise ESS-based stopping rule implemented dcm_sample().
code: mod <- compile_cdcm() stan_dat <- get_stan_dat(data, hypothesis_idxs) init_list <- get_initial_vals(mod, stan_dat)  fit <- mod$sample(   data = stan_dat,   init = init_list,   chains = 4,   parallel_chains = 4,   iter_warmup = 1000,   iter_sampling = 1000,   seed = 1234 )

what: Generate initial values for CDCM posterior sampling — get_initial_vals

text: Produces list initial values CDCM Stan model, optionally using CmdStanR Pathfinder obtain data-informed initializations.

title: Generate initial values for CDCM posterior sampling — get_initial_vals
Generate initial values for CDCM posterior sampling — get_initial_vals
text: mod compiled CmdStanR model object, typically returned compile_cdcm. data named list data format required Stan model, typically produced get_stan_dat. pathfinder_init Optional named list initial values seed Pathfinder run. NULL, default values constructed internally.

title: Generate initial values for CDCM posterior sampling — get_initial_vals
text: list initial values suitable use Stan sampling. Pathfinder succeeds, based final Pathfinder draw. Pathfinder fails, backup initialization list based default values returned instead.

title: Generate initial values for CDCM posterior sampling — get_initial_vals
text: pathfinder_init supplied, default initial values used: observation noise parameters initialized 1, neural connectivity parameters initialized 0, initial neural states initialized 0.1, hemodynamic scaling parameters initialized 0. function attempts run CmdStanR Pathfinder extract final Pathfinder draw format suitable posterior sampling. Pathfinder fails reason, backup initialization list based default values returned instead, message issued. object returned function naturally single-chain initialization list. multi-chain sampling via mod$sample(), users may either: run get_initial_vals() separately generate one initialization list per chain, reuse/replicate single initialization list across chains appropriate analysis.

code: get_stan_dat(   data,   hypothesis_idxs,   ode_solver_type = 1,   tol = 1e-05,   sigma_nu = 1,   sigma_nu_self = 0.125,   rate_sigma = 0.5,   sigma_z0 = 0.3,   conv = 1,   max_num_steps = 10^6 )

title: Construct a Stan data list for the CDCM model — get_stan_dat
text: data list containing observed time series experimental design. must contain: times: numeric vector observation times u: \(T \times n_u\) matrix encoding experimental stimulus inputs y_obs: \(T \times m\) matrix observed ROI-level BOLD signals hypothesis_idxs list specifying hypothesized nonzero entries connectivity matrices. must contain: A_idxs: 2-column matrix \((,j)\) indices baseline connectivity matrix \(\) B_idxs: 3-column matrix \((k,,j)\) indices modulatory matrices \(B_k\), \(k\) indexes stimulus dimension C_idxs: 2-column matrix \((,j)\) indices driving-input matrix \(C\) ode_solver_type Integer code specifying ODE solver used Stan program (default = 1; piecewise analytic solution, faster CKRK numeric integration = 0). tol Numeric tolerance used relative absolute ODE solver tolerances (default = 1e-5; used CKRK numeric integration). sigma_nu Prior scale -diagonal neural connectivity parameters (default = 1). sigma_nu_self Prior scale diagonal self-connectivity parameters (default = 0.125; tighter prior needed). rate_sigma Rate parameter prior observation noise. sigma_z0 Prior scale initial neural state. conv Integer indicator controlling whether convolution HRF performed Stan model (default = 1; leave -unless want latent z(t) instead y(t)). max_num_steps Maximum number ODE solver steps allowed (default = 10^6; CKRK numeric solver ).

title: Construct a Stan data list for the CDCM model — get_stan_dat
text: named list containing data, dimensions, changepoint structure, hypothesis index matrices, prior hyperparameters, solver settings required CDCM Stan program.

title: Construct a Stan data list for the CDCM model — get_stan_dat
text: function validates structure supplied data hypothesis index objects, computes changepoints experimental design, returns named list format required CDCM Stan model. addition, function evaluates set design diagnostics via check_design_identifiability. sufficient conditions satisfied, warning issued. warning intended practical diagnostic : failure satisfy conditions necessarily imply non-identifiability, may indicate design weak reliable estimation. returned Stan data list can used either single-chain multi-chain sampling. structure depend number chains.

title: Path to the group-level meta-analysis Stan program — meta_analysis_stan_file
Path to the group-level meta-analysis Stan program — meta_analysis_stan_file
text: character string giving full path meta_analysis.stan file.

title: Path to the group-level meta-analysis Stan program — meta_analysis_stan_file
text: function primarily intended use cmdstanr::cmdstan_model() compiling model.

title: Path to the group-level meta-analysis Stan program — meta_analysis_stan_file

what: Compute a minimum ESS threshold for CDCM sampling — minESS_criterion
title: Compute a minimum ESS threshold for CDCM sampling — minESS_criterion
text: Computes minimum effective sample size (ESS) threshold corresponding specified confidence level relative precision, using number model parameters implied CDCM Stan data list.

title: Compute a minimum ESS threshold for CDCM sampling — minESS_criterion
Compute a minimum ESS threshold for CDCM sampling — minESS_criterion
text: data named list data format required Stan model, typically produced get_stan_dat. alpha Significance level used mcmcse::minESS() calculation. Defaults 0.05. eps Relative precision target used mcmcse::minESS() calculation. Defaults 0.05.

title: Compute a minimum ESS threshold for CDCM sampling — minESS_criterion
text: numeric scalar giving minimum ESS threshold.

title: Compute a minimum ESS threshold for CDCM sampling — minESS_criterion
text: function first determines number model parameters implied supplied Stan data list, uses mcmcse::minESS() compute minimum effective sample size required achieve specified level alpha relative precision eps. can used define ess_check argument dcm_sample.

title: Compute a minimum ESS threshold for CDCM sampling — minESS_criterion
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ stan_dat <- get_stan_dat(data, hypothesis_idxs) ess_target <- minESS_criterion(stan_dat) } # }

what: Simulate fMRI time series under CDCM — simulate_cdcm
title: Simulate fMRI time series under CDCM — simulate_cdcm
text: Simulates neural state trajectories observed BOLD signals CDCM user-specified connectivity parameters experimental inputs.

title: Simulate fMRI time series under CDCM — simulate_cdcm
code: simulate_cdcm(   nu,   hypothesis_idxs,   nscan,   m,   n_u,   TR,   U,   SNR,   reparam = TRUE,   z0 = NULL,   check_solution = TRUE,   tol = 1e-04,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate fMRI time series under CDCM — simulate_cdcm
text: nu list containing true parameter values: nu_A numeric vector (baseline connectivity) parameters nu_B numeric vector B (modulatory) parameters nu_C numeric vector C (input) parameters hypothesis_idxs list specifying locations nonzero parameters: A_idxs matrix 2 columns (row, column indices) B_idxs matrix 3 columns (input, row, column indices) C_idxs matrix 2 columns (row, input indices) nscan Number scan time points. m Number brain regions (state dimension). n_u Number experimental inputs. TR Repetition time (seconds). U Input (stimulus) matrix dimension nscan x n_u (nscan + 1) x n_u. Values must binary (0/1). SNR Signal--noise ratio additive Gaussian noise. reparam Logical. TRUE, reparameterizes diagonal elements matrix enforce stability. z0 Optional initial state vector. NULL, default used. check_solution Logical. TRUE, compares analytic numeric ODE solutions assess numerical stability. tol Numeric tolerance analytic vs numeric solution comparison. seed Optional seed (scalar length m) reproducible noise generation.

title: Simulate fMRI time series under CDCM — simulate_cdcm
text: list two components: simulated_data times Vector scan times (length nscan) u Input matrix scan times (nscan x n_u) y_obs Observed BOLD signals (nscan x m) true_signals z_signal Latent neural states (nscan x m) y_signal Noise-free BOLD signals (nscan x m)

title: Simulate fMRI time series under CDCM — simulate_cdcm
text: function generates: latent neural states via analytic solution bilinear state equation observed BOLD signals via convolution canonical hemodynamic response function (HRF) additive Gaussian noise controlled specified signal--noise ratio (SNR) user supplies connectivity structure nu hypothesis_idxs, along stimulus design matrix U. Internally, function augments input matrix include initial time point required solving system. neural dynamics follow bilinear state space DCM. Within block constant input, system admits closed-form solution, used efficient simulation. observed BOLD signal obtained convolving neural states canonical HRF (linear combination two gamma distributions).

title: Simulate fMRI time series under CDCM — simulate_cdcm
code: nu <- list(   nu_A = c(0.4, 0.3, -0.1, 0.15),   nu_B = c(-0.2),   nu_C = c(0.7) )  hypothesis_idxs <- list(   A_idxs = matrix(c(2,1,                     1,2,                     1,1,                     2,2), byrow = TRUE, ncol = 2),   B_idxs = matrix(c(1,2,1), byrow = TRUE, ncol = 3),   C_idxs = matrix(c(1,1), byrow = TRUE, ncol = 2) )  U <- matrix(c(0,rep(c(rep(0,5),rep(1,5)), 2)), ncol = 1)  sim <- simulate_cdcm(   nu = nu,   hypothesis_idxs = hypothesis_idxs,   nscan = 20,   m = 2,   n_u = 1,   TR = 2,   U = U,   SNR = 5,   seed = 1 )
